name: Mixed Fruit and Toffee Corn
recipeIngredient: 8 cups Popped popcorn (about 1/3 to 1/2 cup unpopped)
6 oz Pkg. mixed dried fruit bits
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove all unpopped kernels from popped corn.
2. Place popcorn in a buttered 17x12x2-inch baking pan. Keep popcorn warm in a 300 degree oven while making syrup mixture.
3. Butter the sides of a heavy 2-quart saucepan.
4. In the saucepan combine the butter or margarine, sugar, water, and corn syrup. Bring mixture to boiling over medium heat.
5. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon to dissolve the sugar. Avoid splashing the mixture on the sides of the pan.
6. Carefully clip candy thermometer to side of pan.
7. Cook and stir mixture over medium heat till thermometer registers 255 degrees, hard-ball stage (10 to 15 minutes). The mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over the entire surface.
8. Remove saucepan from heat; remove candy thermometer. Quickly add pecans and vanilla; stir until combined.
9. Pour mixture over the popcorn, toss gently to coat.
10. Bake, uncovered, in a 300 degree oven for 15 minutes.
11. Remove popcorn from oven; stir in fruit bits. Bake, uncovered, for 5 more minutes.
12. Turn popcorn mixture onto a large piece of foil. Cool mixture completely.
13. Serve immediately or store, tightly covered, in a cool, dry place. Makes about 22 (1/2 cup) servings.
14. Nutritional information per 1/2 cup serving: 171 cal., 10 g fat, 24mg chol., 1 g pro., 20g carbo., 0g fiber, 105 mg sodium. U.S. RDA: 12% vit. A.
